name: Bolaños de Calatrava from Almagro
name_original: Bolaños de Calatrava desde Almagro – circular por Moral de Calatrava
description: An easy 27.5-mile road cycling route connecting Almagro, Moral de Calatrava, and Bolaños de Calatrava, featuring historical towns and La Man
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Bolaños De Calatrava traverse the flat to gently rolling plains of Castilla-La Mancha, characterized by expansive agricultural landscapes and scattered historical towns. The region offers a network of paved roads connecting villages and providing views of the local countryside. Elevation changes are generally moderate, with some routes featuring gradual ascents and descents. Best road cycling routes around Bolaños De Calatrava The most popular road cycling route is Pozuelo de Calatrava, Miguelturra y Ermita de Santa Marina — circular desde Almagro, a 53.4 miles (85.9 km) trail that takes 3 hours 25 minutes to complete. This moderate route connects several towns and passes by the Ermita de Santa Marina. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Bolaños de Calatrava from Almagro, an easy 27.5 miles (44.3 km) path. This route offers a direct connection between two key towns in the region, featuring open views of the agricultural land. Local road cyclists also love the Bolaños, Torralba y Carrión de Calatrava — circular desde Almagro, a 27.9 miles (45.0 km) trail leading through multiple towns and rural areas, often completed in about 1 hour 43 minutes. Road cycling around Bolaños De Calatrava is defined by open agricultural fields, quiet country roads, and connections between historical villages. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy rides to more moderate distances. The routes in Bolaños De Calatrava are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 10 reviews. More than 350 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Bolaños De Calatrava's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Bolaños De Calatrava?
A: There are over 20 road cycling routes around Bolaños De Calatrava, offering a variety of options for different skill levels and preferences.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Bolaños De Calatrava?
A: The best times for road cycling in Bolaños De Calatrava are typically spring (April to June) and autumn (September to October). During these months, the weather is generally mild and pleasant, avoiding the intense heat of summer and the cooler, sometimes damp, conditions of winter. The region's flat to gently rolling terrain is enjoyable year-round, but these seasons offer optimal comfort.

Q: Are there easy road cycling routes suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, Bolaños De Calatrava offers several easy routes. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1070310263/bolanos-de-calatrava-from-almagro" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bolaños de Calatrava from Almagro&lt;/a&gt; route is an easy 27.5-mile (44.3 km) path that provides open views of agricultural land and connects two key towns, making it suitable for those seeking a less strenuous ride.

Q: What are the road conditions like for cycling in the region?
A: Road cycling routes around Bolaños De Calatrava primarily utilize a network of paved roads connecting villages. These roads generally traverse open agricultural fields and quiet country areas. While conditions are typically good, it's always advisable to check local conditions before heading out, especially after heavy rain.

Q: Are there any longer or more challenging routes for advanced cyclists?
A: For cyclists looking for longer distances, the region offers moderate routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1251905687/pozuelo-de-calatrava-miguelturra-y-ermita-de-santa-marina-circular-desde-almagro" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pozuelo de Calatrava, Miguelturra y Ermita de Santa Marina — circular desde Almagro&lt;/a&gt; route is a moderate 53.4-mile (85.9 km) trail that includes some gradual ascents and descents, connecting several towns and passing by the Ermita de Santa Marina.

Q: What natural attractions can I see along the road cycling routes?
A: The routes often pass through areas with unique natural features. You might encounter volcanic landscapes such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1789437" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerro Gordo Volcano&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6832426" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Yezosa Volcano&lt;/a&gt;. Other natural highlights include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4596459" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Thermal Baths of the Virgen de las Nieves&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4573401" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuente del Caño Cave&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural landmarks to explore while cycling?
A: Yes, the region is dotted with historical towns and landmarks. Many routes connect villages where you can find sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3854730" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of San Pedro (Almagro)&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3569390" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sanctuary of Our Lady of the Snows&lt;/a&gt;, or the iconic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2895336" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Almagro Main Square&lt;/a&gt;. The town of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5685079" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valenzuela de Calatrava&lt;/a&gt; is also a notable settlement along some routes.

Q: Are there circular road cycling routes available?
A: Yes, many routes in the area are circular. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1249647502/bolanos-torralba-y-carrion-de-calatrava-circular-desde-almagro" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bolaños, Torralba y Carrión de Calatrava — circular desde Almagro&lt;/a&gt; is a 27.9-mile (45.0 km) loop that takes you through multiple towns and rural areas. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20129076" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Almagro – Valenzuela de Calatrava loop from Bolaños de Calatrava&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other cyclists say about road cycling in Bolaños De Calatrava?
A: The road cycling routes around Bolaños De Calatrava are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 10 reviews. Cyclists often praise the quiet country roads, the open agricultural landscapes, and the connections between historical villages, which offer a pleasant and varied riding experience.

Q: Are there routes that pass through towns with opportunities for cafe stops?
A: Given the network of roads connecting various historical towns and villages, many routes naturally offer opportunities for cafe stops. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/5298822" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of San Juan Bautista – Villar del Pozo loop from Almagro&lt;/a&gt;, which passes through multiple settlements, are likely to have places where you can pause for refreshments and experience local hospitality.
tips: In the center of the old town. Rectangular, irregular floor plan, formed by two flanks with arcades of Tuscan stone columns under two running galleries, originally public in nature and open to the outside, and currently closed by a Central European style glazing that makes it unique in Spain. Located at the confluence of the main communication routes.

It emerges as a place of social recreation that throughout history has undergone several transformations until reaching its current state.
The surroundings of the Plaza de España is the most important monumental area of Moral. Its four sides are delimited by unique buildings, such as the Parish Church of San Andrés Apóstol, the Town Hall, the old Pósito and some stately homes.
Brief but steep climb that leaves Moral de Calatrava along the CR-5213 road in the shadow of the wind farms that dominate the top of the hillside.

Bolaños de Calatrava is located in the volcanic area of Campo de Calatrava. Reference city in the region is one of the most populated and with a strong economy based on industry and services.

Its monumental castle of Arab origin known as Castillo de Doña Berenguela stands out. The fortress is located in the center of the town, it can be visited and hosts a great cultural and tourist programme. The Hermitage of Cristo de la Columna from the 14th century with a Baroque altarpiece and Gothic paintings on the walls; the Parish Church of San Felipe and Santiago, built in the 16th century; the Mudejar houses of Calle Cristo; the noble house of Coca; the Casino de la Verja and the hermitages of the Virgen del Monte make up the most important visiting areas that arouse great interest.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route is circular and starts from Almagro. You can typically find parking in or around the town center of Almagro, which is a good starting point to explore the area before or after your ride.

faqs.question: How challenging is the terrain on this route?
faqs.answer: While komoot rates this route as 'easy' for racebike, the terrain in the Campo de Calatrava region is generally undulating. This means you can expect some gentle slopes and rolling hills, making it a moderately challenging ride that may require good fitness, especially for the full circular distance.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the way?
faqs.answer: This route offers a blend of cultural heritage and typical La Mancha landscapes. You'll pass through historical towns with notable features such as the unique Almagro Main Square and the Plaza de España. Further along, you might encounter the Climb to the Wind Turbines of El Moral and the historic Castillo de Doña Berenguela in Bolaños de Calatrava.

faqs.question: Is this a circular cycling route?
faqs.answer: Yes, this is a circular route that connects Almagro, Moral de Calatrava, and Bolaños de Calatrava, bringing you back to your starting point in Almagro.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The Campo de Calatrava region can experience hot summers. Therefore, the best times to cycle this route are typically spring (April to June) and autumn (September to October) when temperatures are milder and more pleasant for outdoor activities.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this route?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle this route, as it primarily uses public roads and passes through towns. The Campo de Calatrava is a comarca (region), not a national park with controlled access.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the route itself is on public roads, bringing a dog on a racebike can be challenging. If you plan to stop in towns, be aware of local regulations regarding dogs in public spaces and establishments. It's generally not recommended for dogs to accompany cyclists on racebike routes of this distance.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other cycling trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, while primarily a road cycling route, it does briefly intersect with sections of other local paths. You might find yourself on small portions of trails like the Ruta de los Navajos y el Volcán de la Estrella, Motilla del Azuer - Moral de Calatrava, Camino de la Quebrada, Camino de Bocapucheros, Cañada de Añavete, and Callejón de León.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or drink along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, as the route passes through the towns of Almagro, Moral de Calatrava, and Bolaños de Calatrava, you will find various cafes, restaurants, and shops where you can refuel and hydrate.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect while cycling?
faqs.answer: You can expect a blend of historical architecture and classic La Mancha rural landscapes. The route takes you through agricultural lands, olive groves, and possibly vineyards, characteristic of the Castile-La Mancha region, with open and expansive views.

faqs.question: Is this route suitable for beginner road cyclists?
faqs.answer: Given its distance of approximately 44 km and undulating terrain, this route is better suited for cyclists with some experience. While not extremely steep, the length and rolling hills might be challenging for absolute beginners on a racebike.
